message:
fix(pager): align 3 tests with current bump-allocator + first-committer-wins semantics

Three pager tests were written against older semantics that have since
been intentionally changed:

- test_dirty_bitmap_double_write_dedups_wal_entry and
  test_dirty_bitmap_commit_flushes_all_dirty_pages asserted that every
  commit emits a Page-1 metadata frame in the WAL frame stream.
  Commit 0a3e90fb (bd-3wop3.8) narrowed requires_page_one_rewrite to
  only fire when Page 1 is EXPLICITLY dirty — synthetic db_growth no
  longer emits a Page-1 header frame, eliminating ~2000 MVCC conflicts
  per 16-thread benchmark iteration. Flipped the assertions to expect
  0 synthetic Page-1 frames and renamed the case labels accordingly.

- test_memory_db_allocator_stays_bump_only_after_rollback used
  TransactionMode::Concurrent for the abandoned transaction, whose
  second allocate_page takes the already_allocated branch and batches
  PAGE_LEASE_BATCH_SIZE (=8) pages via lease. The old assertion
  computed `expected = abandoned_pages[1] + 1` which silently assumed
  batch=1. Updated `expected = abandoned_pages[0] + 1 + PAGE_LEASE_BATCH_SIZE`
  to correctly reflect the lease-advance, with a comment explaining
  why Concurrent mode is required (Immediate mode's rollback resets
  next_page, which would be a weaker check of the bump-only invariant).

Verified in isolation:
  test_dirty_bitmap                                    8 pass 0 fail
  test_memory_db_allocator_stays_bump_only_after_rollback  ok

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
